characters.name: Marcus
characters.physicalDescription: Weathered hands, crooked nose from a break that never healed right. Gray at the temples. Moves like someone who learned to take up less space than he occupies.
characters.backstory: Former boxer turned bartender. Left the ring after accidentally blinding an opponent. Carries the guilt like a physical weight.
characters.selfNarrative: Believes he's a reformed man. Believes the violence is behind him.
contradictions: Still sizes up every man who walks through the door
Keeps his hands wrapped when alone

voice.vocabularyNotes: Blue-collar precise. Uses trade terms from boxing and bartending. Never reaches for a fancy word when a plain one works. Avoids abstractions.
dialogueSamples: You ever notice how the ice never melts the same way twice?
That's not what I said. That's what you heard.
Some nights the bar closes itself. You just have to notice.

behavior.stressResponse: Goes still, speaks slower, hands find something to clean
behavior.socialPosture: Occupies the edge of conversations, draws people out
behavior.noticesFirst: Hands. Always looks at someone's hands first.
behavior.lyingStyle: Omits rather than fabricates. Changes the subject with a question.
behavior.emotionPhysicality: Jaw tightens. Hands go to pockets or find a glass to polish. Never raises his voice — gets quieter instead.

characters.name: Elena
characters.physicalDescription: Sharp cheekbones, dark eyes that catalogue everything. Thin silver ring she turns when thinking. Dresses like she has somewhere better to be.
characters.backstory: Journalist who got too close to a story and lost her editor's trust. Here because Marcus is the only source who never lied to her.

voice.vocabularyNotes: Educated, precise. Uses longer sentences when deflecting, shorter when genuine. Occasional journalistic jargon slips through.
verbalTics: uses people's names mid-sentence as emphasis
dialogueSamples: Marcus. That's not an answer and we both know it.
Three sources, all independent, all saying the same thing.

sentenceArchitecture.targetVariance: High variance — alternate between 4-word punches and 20-word observations
sentenceArchitecture.fragmentPolicy: Fragments allowed for rhythm. Max 2 consecutive.
sentenceArchitecture.notes: Avoid three consecutive sentences of similar length.

paragraphPolicy.singleSentenceFrequency: Every 3rd or 4th paragraph should be a single sentence
paragraphPolicy.notes: Dialogue paragraphs exempt from length rules

killList.pattern: he didn't know what to say

negativeExemplars.text: She felt a wave of sadness wash over her as she realized the magnitude of what had happened.
negativeExemplars.annotation: Telling not showing. Named emotion. Cliché metaphor (wave/wash).

positiveExemplars.text: The glass sweated in his grip. He set it down too carefully, the way you handle something you're thinking about throwing.
positiveExemplars.annotation: Embodied detail. Subtext through physical action. Tension without naming it.

structuralBans: Never end a paragraph on a stated emotion
Never have a character explain their own motivations aloud
Never use weather to establish mood unless the weather is plot-relevant

pov.notes: Marcus only. We see everything through his perception but never get direct thought transcription.

narrativeRules.subtextPolicy: Characters never state the actual topic of conversation. Subtext must remain sub.
narrativeRules.expositionPolicy: Drip-feed only. No character explains backstory unprompted. Information emerges through implication and half-statements.
narrativeRules.sceneEndingPolicy: End on action or image, never on reflection or summary. The last sentence should be concrete.

locations.name: The Bar
locations.description: A dive bar that's outlived three owners and two neighborhoods. Not charming — enduring.

sounds: the low hum of a dying cooler
smells: cigarette ghost from the ban era
textures: sticky lacquer on the bar top
cracked leather on the corner booth
sensoryPalette.lightQuality: Amber from neon beer signs filtered through years of nicotine film on the windows. Pools of light, not general illumination.
sensoryPalette.atmosphere: A place where silence is comfortable and conversation is optional
